question: How many times can a student repeat a failed exam at KU Eichstätt-Ingolstadt (excluding the bachelor's thesis)?
ground_truth: A student can repeat a failed exam twice. The exam can be repeated a maximum of two times, excluding the bachelor's or master's thesis.

question: What duration range is specified for an oral exam at KU Eichstätt-Ingolstadt?
ground_truth: An oral exam should not be shorter than 15 minutes or longer than 60 minutes.

question: How many ECTS points is the bachelor's thesis worth at KU Eichstätt-Ingolstadt?
ground_truth: The bachelor's thesis is worth 10 ECTS points.

question: What overall grade average is required to receive the distinction 'mit Auszeichnung' (with distinction) in the bachelor's or master's exam at KU?
ground_truth: A final grade of 1.20 or better is required to receive the distinction 'mit Auszeichnung' (with distinction).

question: When is the earliest a student can receive the topic for their bachelor's thesis?
ground_truth: The topic of the bachelor's thesis can be issued at the earliest when mandatory or required elective modules worth at least 60 percent of the total ECTS points of the program have been successfully completed.

question: Within what time period must a student submit a request for file inspection (Akteneinsicht) after a grade is announced?
ground_truth: The request for file inspection must be submitted within one month after the grade is announced.

question: What happens if the deadline for completing the bachelor's or master's exam is exceeded by more than two semesters for reasons the student is responsible for?
ground_truth: The bachelor's or master's exam is considered definitively failed (endgültig nicht bestanden).

question: What percentage of a course may a student miss and still fulfill the attendance requirement at KU Eichstätt-Ingolstadt?
ground_truth: A student may miss no more than 25 percent of the course sessions and still fulfill the attendance requirement.

question: According to the 2022 amended APO, how is it treated if an exam is written wholly or partly by someone else on behalf of the author, including through the use of artificial intelligence (AI)?
ground_truth: It is treated as deception (Täuschung), specifically ghostwriting: the rule applies when someone writes all or part of the exam in another person’s name and on their behalf, including when artificial intelligence is used.

question: What is the Studium.Pro program at KU and how many areas does it have?
ground_truth: Studium.Pro is an interdisciplinary study offering at KU that complements a student's main program and enables the acquisition of competencies reflecting a culture of academic responsibility. It is divided into five areas: Pro Diskurs, Pro Horizont, Pro Gesellschaft, Pro Beruf, and Pro International.

question: When an electronic remote exam (elektronische Fernprüfung) is offered, must an in-person alternative also be provided?
ground_truth: Yes. When an electronic remote exam is held, the same exam must also be offered in person (in Präsenzform) during the same examination period.

question: What is a Take-Home-Prüfung and how is it conducted according to the 2022 APO?
ground_truth: A Take-Home-Prüfung is an exam in electronic form that does not take place in person at KU, but is instead completed at home without supervision within a specified time frame. Exam materials are issued and submitted electronically. Limited aids may be permitted.

question: How long is the text portion of a Portfolio exam supposed to be according to the 2022 amended APO?
ground_truth: The text portion of a Portfolio typically comprises approximately 18,000 to 30,000 characters without spaces, depending on the ECTS points specified in the program regulations.

question: According to the 2022 APO, what happens to the exam attempt if a technical failure occurs during an electronic remote exam?
ground_truth: If a technical failure occurs, the exam is ended at the current point and the exam attempt is considered as not having taken place (not counted). This does not apply if the disruption is proven to be within the student's own area of responsibility.

question: According to the 2022 amended APO, how is carrying mobile devices and digital storage media during an exam treated?
ground_truth: Carrying mobile devices and digital storage media is treated as an attempt to cheat (Täuschungsversuch).

question: How many total ECTS points must a student earn to complete the Digital & Data-Driven Business bachelor's program at KU?
ground_truth: A student must earn a total of 180 ECTS points to complete the Digital & Data-Driven Business bachelor's program.

question: What academic degree is awarded upon completing the Digital & Data-Driven Business program at KU Eichstätt-Ingolstadt?
ground_truth: Graduates receive the degree of Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.).

question: How many ECTS points is the Digital Project worth in the D3B program, and what is the maximum time horizon to complete it?
ground_truth: The Digital Project is worth 10 ECTS points and represents 2 working months within a maximum time horizon of 9 months.

question: Which four study profiles (Studienprofile) are generally offered in the Digital & Data-Driven Business program?
ground_truth: The four study profiles generally offered are: Accounting, Taxation & Controlling; Finance & Economics; Marketing, Organization, Innovation; and Supply Chain Management & Logistics.

question: How many ECTS points must a student complete within a specific study profile for it to appear on their final certificate (Zeugnis)?
ground_truth: A student must successfully complete at least 30 ECTS points within a specific study profile for it to be listed on their final certificate.

question: Which faculty is primarily responsible for the Digital & Data-Driven Business program, and which faculty also participates?
ground_truth: The Wirtschaftswissenschaftliche Fakultät (Faculty of Business and Economics) is primarily responsible, with the Mathematisch-Geographische Fakultät (Faculty of Mathematics and Geography) also participating.

question: Which study semester is especially suitable for studying abroad in the D3B program, and why?
ground_truth: The fifth study semester is especially suitable for studying abroad because no modules are assigned to it that would be difficult to study abroad except with considerable effort.

question: What are the four core subject areas that the Digital & Data-Driven Business program combines?
ground_truth: The four core subject areas are: (1) Information processing systems, methods and concepts; (2) Business and economics with a focus on the digital economy; (3) Quantitative methods; and (4) Language, social, communication and action competence (soft skills) and ethics.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, what is required for taking university examinations at KU?
ground_truth: Taking university examinations requires enrollment or matriculation according to Art. 87 Abs. 1 Satz 1 BayHIG, unless a cooperation agreement with another university allows students to participate in courses and complete study or examination achievements without enrollment at KU.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, what must be included with written examination work completed without supervision?
ground_truth: Written examination work completed without supervision, such as term papers, study papers, portfolios and similar work, must include a list of the aids used. Content taken directly or indirectly from publications or other sources must be marked as such.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, who decides which aids may be used in an examination?
ground_truth: The examiner decides and informs students in good time which aids may be used in the examination.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, under what condition is the use of artificial intelligence not treated as deception?
ground_truth: The use of artificial intelligence is not treated as deception if AI is permitted as an aid under the conditions of § 12 Abs. 7 or § 16 Abs. 6.

question: What special section was added to the APO by the 2024 amendment?
ground_truth: A new section was added for special provisions for university of applied sciences degree programs, including practical study semesters and special rules for examination bodies.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, what is a practical study semester?
ground_truth: A practical study semester is an integrated, university-regulated, supervised study semester with defined content, usually completed in a company or other professional practice institution outside the university.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, how long does a practical study semester usually last if the program regulations do not specify otherwise?
ground_truth: It usually lasts a continuous period of 22 weeks, including accompanying courses.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, what happens if a second examination date lies in the following semester and using it would exceed the maximum study duration?
ground_truth: The maximum study duration is extended automatically by one semester.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, when is withdrawal from a semester-accompanying examination no longer allowed?
ground_truth: For semester-accompanying examinations, starting the examination performance, such as submitting a paper or giving a presentation, counts as registration, and withdrawal is no longer allowed from that point.

question: According to the 2024 APO amendment, how is the grade determined if two examiners evaluate an examination differently and cannot agree?
ground_truth: If two examiners cannot agree on a common evaluation, the grade is calculated as the arithmetic mean rounded down to one decimal place.

question: Which four exemplary study profiles are shown in the D3B study plan document?
ground_truth: The D3B study plan document shows four exemplary study profiles: Accounting, Taxation and Controlling; Finance and Economics; Marketing, Organization, Innovation; and Supply Chain Management and Logistics.

question: How many ECTS are planned for each semester in the exemplary D3B study plans?
ground_truth: Each semester in the exemplary D3B study plans is planned with 30 ECTS.

question: In the Accounting, Taxation and Controlling study profile, how many ECTS is the Bachelor thesis worth?
ground_truth: In the Accounting, Taxation and Controlling study profile, the Bachelor thesis is worth 10 ECTS.

question: In the Finance and Economics study profile, in which semester is the Bachelor thesis shown?
ground_truth: In the Finance and Economics study profile, the Bachelor thesis is shown in the sixth semester.

question: Which study profile includes the module Digital & Data Project in Marketing, Organization, Innovation?
ground_truth: The module Digital & Data Project in Marketing, Organization, Innovation is included in the Marketing, Organization, Innovation study profile.

question: Which study profile includes Digital Project in SCM, Logistics and Operations Research?
ground_truth: Digital Project in SCM, Logistics and Operations Research is included in the Supply Chain Management and Logistics study profile.

question: Which common modules appear in the first two semesters of the D3B study plans?
ground_truth: Common early modules include Grundlagen: Informationssysteme, Mathe für WiWi, Betriebliches Rechnungswesen, Digital Business Models, Einf. VWL, Business English I, Business Analytics, Einführung Quantitative Methoden, Statistik I, Digital Business Strategies, Organisation, Innovation, Mikroökonomie, and Business English II.

question: What do bold and italic module names indicate in the D3B study plan document?
ground_truth: Bold module names indicate elective modules, and italic module names indicate elective or study-profile modules.

question: Which module is shown as a 10 ECTS project in the Finance and Economics study profile?
ground_truth: The Finance and Economics study profile shows Digital & Data Project in Finance & Economics as a 10 ECTS project.

question: Which study profile includes the module Digital Seminar in Data Science & Quantitative Applications?
ground_truth: The module Digital Seminar in Data Science & Quantitative Applications appears in the Marketing, Organization, Innovation profile and in the Supply Chain Management and Logistics profile.

question: Which elective areas are listed in the SoSe 2026 D3B elective module catalogue?
ground_truth: The SoSe 2026 D3B elective module catalogue lists the elective areas Digitalization & Analytics, Data Competence, Application Competence, Business Language and Management Skills, and Wirtschafts- und Unternehmensethik.

question: How many ECTS is the module Digital Seminar in Data Science & Quantitative Applications worth in the SoSe 2026 elective module catalogue?
ground_truth: The module Digital Seminar in Data Science & Quantitative Applications is worth 10 ECTS.

question: Which modules are listed in the Data Competence elective area in the SoSe 2026 catalogue?
ground_truth: The Data Competence elective area lists Algorithmen und Datenstrukturen, Rechnergestützte Statistik mit R, and Hands-on Machine Learning and Data Science.

question: What examination form is listed for Business English I in the SoSe 2026 elective catalogue?
ground_truth: Business English I has the examination form Klausur plus mündliche Prüfung.

question: What prerequisite is listed for Business English II in the SoSe 2026 elective catalogue?
ground_truth: Business English II requires Business English I.

question: How many ECTS must students complete from a study profile catalogue for the profile to be shown on the certificate?
ground_truth: Students must successfully complete at least 30 ECTS from a study profile catalogue for the profile to be shown on the certificate.

question: Which modules are listed in the Supply Chain Management and Logistics study profile in the SoSe 2026 catalogue?
ground_truth: The Supply Chain Management and Logistics profile lists Digital Seminar in Data Science & Quantitative Applications, Retail Management Fundamentals, Systementwicklung, Operations Analytics, Supply Chain Analytics, SCM Projektstudium, and Internat. Transportlogistik und Distributionssysteme (VHB).

question: Which module in the Supply Chain Management and Logistics profile is worth 10 ECTS?
ground_truth: Digital Seminar in Data Science & Quantitative Applications is worth 10 ECTS.

question: Which modules are listed in the Sustainability in Business and Economics study profile?
ground_truth: The Sustainability in Business and Economics profile lists Ringvorlesung: Nachhaltige Wirtschaft, Innovating for Sustainability: Frameworks and Practices from Global and Local Organizations, Sustainable Entrepreneurship, Umweltökonomie & Umweltpolitik, Öffentliche Finanzen und nachhaltige Wirtschaftspolitik, Sustainable Development, and Company Taxation in the EU: Towards more Fairness and Sustainability.

question: Which modules are listed in the Application Competence elective area in the SoSe 2026 catalogue?
ground_truth: The Application Competence elective area lists Steuerbilanzen und Rechtsformwahl, Marketing and Management, Operations Analytics, and Kapitalmarkttheorie.
